title: A Cry
content:
Oh, there are eyes that he can see,
And hands to make his hands rejoice,
But to my lover I must be
Only a voice.

Oh, there are breasts to bear his head,
And lips whereon his lips can lie,
But I must be till I am dead
Only a cry.
meaning:
Sara Teasdale's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Cry"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
